description: Fix layout alignment and spacing inconsistencies in the Chrome extension side panel UI to achieve pixel-perfect visual polish across plugin cards, tool rows, search bar, and footer.

userStories.title: Align tool rows and tab state hints with plugin name in accordion cards
userStories.description: As a user viewing the side panel, I want tool rows, tab state hints, and filter count text to be left-aligned with the plugin display name so that the card content reads as a cohesive visual hierarchy.
acceptanceCriteria: Tool row left padding aligns tool icons with plugin icons (both start at the same horizontal position within the card)
TabStateHint text left padding matches tool row left padding
Filter count ('X of Y tools') left padding matches tool row left padding
All three elements (tab state hint, filter count, tool rows) share the same pl value
The left padding value is px-3 (matching the accordion trigger's px-3), so tool icons and plugin icons start at the same left offset within the card
userStories.notes:
Three locations need the same change — replace `pl-5` with `pl-3` to match the accordion trigger header's `px-3`:

1. `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ToolRow.tsx:24` — change `pr-3 pl-5` to `px-3`
2. `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx:31` (TabStateHint closed state) — change `py-2 pr-3 pl-5` to `px-3 py-2`
3. `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx:37` (TabStateHint unavailable state) — change `py-2 pr-3 pl-5` to `px-3 py-2`
4. `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx:149` (filter count) — change `pt-2 pr-3 pl-5` to `px-3 pt-2`

The accordion trigger (PluginCard.tsx:109) uses `px-3 py-2`, which sets the card's established left padding. All content within the accordion body should use the same left padding so the plugin icon and tool icons share the same left edge. This creates a clean left-aligned column down the entire card.

userStories.title: Fix footer horizontal padding to match content area
userStories.description: As a user viewing the side panel, I want the footer bar to be horizontally aligned with the content above it so the panel reads as a single cohesive column.
acceptanceCriteria: Footer horizontal padding is px-3 (matching App.tsx main content area and search bar padding)
Footer left edge visually aligns with the search bar and plugin card content above
userStories.notes:
In `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/Footer.tsx:9`, change `px-4` to `px-3`.

The rest of the panel uses `px-3` consistently:
- App.tsx:200 search bar wrapper: `px-3 py-2`
- App.tsx:220 main content: `flex-1 px-3 py-2`
- PluginCard.tsx:109 trigger: `px-3 py-2`

The footer's `px-4` is the only element using 16px horizontal padding instead of 12px. Changing to `px-3` aligns it with everything else.

userStories.title: Remove shadow-xs override from search input so shadow matches plugin cards
userStories.description: As a user viewing the side panel, I want the search bar to have the same visual weight as the plugin cards below it so the UI reads as a consistent set of floating elements.
acceptanceCriteria: Search input uses the Input component's default shadow-md (no shadow override in className)
On focus, the search input transitions from shadow-md to shadow-xs (the Input component's built-in focus behavior), providing visible feedback
The search input's resting shadow matches the accordion card shadows below it
userStories.notes:
In `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx:207`, the Input's className is `pr-8 pl-9 shadow-xs`. Remove `shadow-xs` so it becomes `pr-8 pl-9`.

The Input component (`platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Input.tsx:17`) has `shadow-md` as its default and `focus:shadow-xs` for the focus state. The `shadow-xs` override in App.tsx makes the input visually lighter than the plugin cards below (which use `shadow-md` from Accordion.tsx:15) and also breaks the focus transition since focus:shadow-xs == shadow-xs (no change).

Removing the override restores the correct shadow hierarchy: resting = shadow-md (matches cards), focused = shadow-xs (visible feedback).

userStories.title: Clean up redundant zero-padding in AccordionContent
userStories.description: As a developer reading the Accordion component code, I want the AccordionContent inner div to not have redundant zero-padding classes that add noise without effect.
acceptanceCriteria: AccordionContent inner div uses cn(className) instead of cn('px-0 pt-0 pb-0', className)
No visual change (zero padding is already the default)
userStories.notes:
In `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Accordion.tsx:50`, change: `
{children}
` to: `
{children}
` Tailwind elements have 0 padding by default. The explicit `px-0 pt-0 pb-0` classes are redundant — they compile to the same CSS as having no padding classes at all. Removing them eliminates noise without any visual change.

userStories.title: Fix AccordionHeader default padding to match established pattern
userStories.description: As a developer using the Accordion design system, I want AccordionHeader's default padding to match the established px-3 pattern used by PluginCard, so the reusable component produces correct layout if used.
acceptanceCriteria: AccordionHeader trigger uses px-3 instead of px-4
No visual change to the current side panel (AccordionHeader is not used by PluginCard, which builds its own header)
userStories.notes:
In `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Accordion.tsx:31`, the AccordionHeader trigger uses `px-4 py-2`. Change `px-4` to `px-3`.

The established horizontal padding for accordion content is `px-3`, set by PluginCard.tsx:109 and matching App.tsx's content area. The reusable AccordionHeader should default to this same value. Currently PluginCard builds its own header with px-3 (because it needs the switch toggle), but if AccordionHeader is ever used directly, it would produce misaligned content with its current px-4.

This is a one-word change: `px-4` → `px-3` on line 31.
